name: Portsmouth Square

permits.description: priority processing ab-004. demolition of existing facilities exposing existing garage roof structure. installation of new waterproofing over existing garage roof structure. construction of new clubhouse and landscape/playground elements. miscellaneous offsite sidewalk and crosswalk improvements.

renov of an(e)parking garage. upgrades to(e)mech,elec,plumb & lighting systems; ada upgrades; waterproofing improvements & repair of(e)structure; reolocation of interior prog areas; replace interior stairs; enclosure of exterior stairs & mech openings; infill of interior structural bay (floor slab)
repair and replacement of (3) passenger elevators that serve the (e) portsmouth square parking garage under declaration of emergency per board of supervisors resolution 455-23
to erect 1 story, no basement, public restrooms.
erect a one story recreation bldg

remove & replace exterior cladding, remove & reinstall aluminum doors & windows w/new flashing & water proofing. remove pavers & concrete slab & install new waterproofing & concrete, remove soil at planters, install drain & waterproofing.

summary_en: Portsmouth Square's record runs from the city's historic heart to a current rebuild. A 1940 WPA theatre history places the city's first schoolhouse and earliest English-language entertainment on Portsmouth Plaza. 1959 Recreation and Park Commission minutes note the square's use for Chinese New Year and a contemplated subterranean garage beneath it. A 1975 Open Space fund report allocates $15,000 for benches and shelters, completed in 1977. A 1984 EIR for 505 Montgomery Street finds the tower would shade up to 40% of the square. The square is operated by the Recreation and Park Department (FY2025 budget line about $250M); its capital record includes a $43,000,000 permit (2026) for a new clubhouse, plus Arts Commission public-art contracts ($30,248 and $10,625 paid).

documents.gloss: A 1984 environmental impact report for the 505 Montgomery Street tower, analyzing the shadows it would cast on Portsmouth Square — up to about 40% of the square in some months.
